name: Porto de Pindo — circle via the Ezaro Viewpoint
name_original: Porto de Pindo — círculo por el Mirador de Ezaro
region_intro: Jogging around Carnota offers diverse terrain along Galicia's Costa da Morte, characterized by its extensive coastline and the prominent Monte Pindo. The region features long sandy beaches, a unique system of dunes and marshes, and rugged mountain trails. These natural features provide varied running surfaces, from flat coastal paths to more challenging ascents, catering to different preferences. Best jogging routes around Carnota The most popular jogging route is Porto de Pindo — circle via the Ezaro Viewpoint, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail that takes about 35 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes near the Ézaro area. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Monte Louro loop from Área de Autocaravanas do Ancoradoiro, a moderate 2.9 miles (4.7 km) path. This path provides scenic views of Monte Louro and the surrounding coastal landscape. Local runners also love the Ézaro Beach – Ézaro Waterfall loop from O Sinal, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail leading through Ézaro Beach and the famous Ézaro Waterfall, often completed in about 55 minutes. Jogging around Carnota is defined by long sandy beaches, coastal trails, and the slopes of Monte Pindo. The network offers options for various ability levels, from flat beach runs to more demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Carnota are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 600 runners have used komoot to explore Carnota's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Carnota?
A: You'll find nearly 50 diverse running routes around Carnota, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 600 times, highlighting the region's popularity for outdoor activities.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Carnota?
A: Carnota offers a wide variety of terrain for runners. You can enjoy long, flat stretches on the expansive &lt;a href="https://visitacostadamorte.com/en/post/beach-de-carnota" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carnota Beach&lt;/a&gt;, which is ideal for long-distance jogging with sea views. There are also coastal paths, unique dune systems, and more challenging, rugged trails on &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3859881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Pindo&lt;/a&gt;, often referred to as the 'Celtic Olympus'.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in Carnota?
A: Yes, Carnota offers some easier options, including one specifically rated as easy. Many of the coastal and beach routes provide relatively flat surfaces, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. The extensive &lt;a href="https://visitacostadamorte.com/en/post/beach-de-carnota" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carnota Beach&lt;/a&gt;, with its 7 kilometers of fine sand, is an excellent choice for a gentle, scenic run.

Q: Can I find challenging trail running routes with significant elevation in Carnota?
A: Absolutely. For runners seeking a challenge, the trails around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3859881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Pindo&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gains and rugged terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20157990" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moa Summit (Monte Pindo) loop from O Fieiro&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult, featuring over 450 meters of ascent over approximately 8.7 kilometers, providing a rewarding experience for advanced trail runners.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by the Ézaro Waterfall?
A: Yes, you can definitely incorporate the unique &lt;a href="https://visitacostadamorte.com/en/concellos/carnota" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; into your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31877109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Beach – Ézaro Waterfall loop from O Sinal&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 8.6 km route that takes you past this spectacular waterfall, which is Europe's only one to plunge directly into the sea.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints along the running routes in Carnota?
A: Carnota boasts several stunning viewpoints. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3859881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moa Summit (Monte Pindo)&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic 360-degree views of the Atlantic Ocean and surrounding landscape. Additionally, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984986182/porto-de-pindo-circle-via-the-ezaro-viewpoint" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porto de Pindo — circle via the Ezaro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; provide coastal vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507602" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Pico da Madalena&lt;/a&gt; is another notable spot.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Carnota?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Carnota are designed as circular loops, offering varied scenery without retracing your steps. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984986182/porto-de-pindo-circle-via-the-ezaro-viewpoint" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porto de Pindo — circle via the Ezaro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; (5.8 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6970994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Monte Louro loop from Área de Autocaravanas do Ancoradoiro&lt;/a&gt; (4.7 km).

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Carnota?
A: The running routes in Carnota are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from the expansive sandy beaches to the rugged mountain trails, and the breathtaking coastal views, especially around areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Louro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://visitacostadamorte.com/en/post/beach-de-carnota" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carnota Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks I can see while running in Carnota?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, Carnota offers cultural points of interest. You can incorporate a visit to one of Galicia's largest and most famous &lt;em&gt;hórreos&lt;/em&gt; (traditional raised granaries) into your exploratory runs. The region also features archaeological sites with Bronze Age petroglyphs, providing an 'open-air museum' experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Carnota?
A: Carnota's mild climate makes it suitable for running throughout much of the year. However, the spring and autumn months often provide the most comfortable temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer can be pleasant, especially for coastal runs, but be mindful of sun exposure. Winter offers a rugged beauty, though some mountain trails might be more challenging due to weather conditions.

Q: Are there any running routes near the Marismas de Caldebarcos for birdwatching?
A: Yes, the Marismas de Caldebarcos wetlands are an excellent spot for birdwatching and can be accessed via nearby trails. While specific running routes directly through the marshes might be limited to protect the ecosystem, you can find paths that run alongside, allowing you to observe diverse bird species like cormorants, gannets, and oystercatchers.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of Monte Louro?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6970994" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Monte Louro loop from Área de Autocaravanas do Ancoradoiro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.7 km path specifically designed to provide scenic views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/507601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Louro&lt;/a&gt; and the surrounding coastal landscape, making it a favorite among local runners.
tips: A spectacular natural spectacle. Very impressive!

Ézaro Beach, located in the parish of O Ézaro (municipality of Dumbría, A Coruña), is one of the natural gems of the Costa da Morte in Galicia. It is especially known for its proximity to the famous Xallas River waterfall, which flows directly into the sea, a unique phenomenon in Europe.
